subject: Re: can't import tilt series from IMOD
body:
Hi Juha,
I had similar problems before.
Indeed, it is better to change the metric system of your Windows, to the American standard. Then decimals are separated by points and thousands by commas.
Many WARP problems just disappear after the change,
Saludos,
Julio

Dr. Julio Ortiz
EM Facility Manager
Forschungszentrum Jülich GmbH
Ernst Ruska-Centrum für Mikroskopie und Spektroskopie
mit Elektronen
ER-C-3: Strukturbiologie
52425 Jülich
Germany
Phone: +49 2461 61-2032
Fax:       +49 2461 61-1448
EMAIL: j.ortiz@fz-juelich.de

Am 26.02.2021 um 09:08 schrieb Juha Huiskonen <juha.huiskonen@gmail.com>:

﻿

Hi All,

I have been able to follow the Warp tutorial to the point where the tomostar files are created based on IMOD alignment:

-  I import the tilt series and alignment information to Warp

- I see the tick box appearing, indicating that alignment was found from the IMOD folders.

- The Tomostar files are created and they point to raw frames that are in the same directory.

However, nothing happens when I click start processing (the circle indicating the number of items to process does not appear).

Any idea why this could happen and what should I do next?

best wishes,

Juha

--
